nodes.label: The root disposition: substance over hype
nodes.one_line: Reduce any hyped category to its substrate, then evaluate

nodes.label: Agent-first thesis
nodes.one_line: Platforms that cannot talk to autonomous agents are already behind; the prompting skill layer migrated, the verdict held

nodes.label: Voice AI craft — cost, latency, scale
nodes.one_line: Voice AI is a distinct engineering discipline with its own constraints; the under-share is deliberate

nodes.label: Breadth as differentiation
nodes.one_line: Breadth differentiates; depth is what makes it matter

nodes.label: PM taste — craft, metaphors, and what a PM actually does
nodes.one_line: PM is taste of what to build, what experience, what value: invisible when done well, irreplaceable in the AI era

nodes.label: What AI PM actually requires
nodes.one_line: LLMs are a primary daily tool; AI replaces the grunt; taste of what to build is irreducible PM work

nodes.label: Enterprise AI reality — ships vs demos
nodes.one_line: Roughly 80% of enterprise AI experiments don't reach production; the gap between demo and deployment is wider than vendors admit

nodes.label: Second brain — structured knowledge for agents
nodes.one_line: Built this for me, hobbyist scope, Karpathy-inspired wiki version. Pattern probably generalizes if you tinker with AI tools daily

nodes.label: Spec first, taste always — the builder trilogy
nodes.one_line: Spec > Sprint. Taste > Execution. Context > Prompt. Lived this since 2021. Named it in April 2026. Same disposition, sharper words

nodes.label: Career reflection — IC path, learning, market lift
nodes.one_line: IC path is legitimate; AI-era amplifies IC leverage; learning compounds when you study concepts not tools; help the market flourish

nodes.label: LinkedIn as instrument — the meta-platform game
nodes.one_line: LinkedIn is a platform to be gamed productively; posting IS the thinking; the loop closes when others learn

nodes.label: Personal projects — ship it or it doesn't exist
nodes.one_line: Ideas are cheap; the prototype is the argument; the loop closes when posted (build → post → teach → learn → build)

nodes.label: Reduce any hyped category to its substrate, then evaluate. 8-year through-line.
Start thinking agent-first — both building and serving lens. Platforms that cannot talk to autonomous agents are behind.
Context > Prompt. Personal and organizational context is the lever; prompt string is the I/O seam.
Spec > Sprint. When you have specced every pixel, a generative tool gives you a worse version of what you already decided.
Taste > Execution. In an AI era that commoditizes execution, the bottleneck and differentiator is taste.
Extremely opinionated against customization settings in enterprise products. Each one signals a design decision deferred to the user. Scope: enterprise (consumer untested).
PM is the grind of answering 99 "should we?" to get to that 1 "can we?".
AI fluency is table stakes for PMs; relevant archetype is application-layer (using models to unlock growth), not foundation-layer. AI replaces the grunt; taste is irreducible.
Strive to learn concepts, not tools. Frameworks endure; tools rotate.
LLMs are a great tool. I use them daily more than any other tool in my stack. (Renamed from llm-as-voice-extension; reframed as factual daily-use claim, not "extension of self.")
Roughly 80% of enterprise AI experiments don't reach production. The gap between demo and deployment is wider than vendors admit. (Frame retained; specific 10-to-2 number dropped at lock.)
Personal context layer for AI sessions. Built this for me, hobbyist scope, Karpathy-inspired. Pattern probably generalizes for builders working with AI tools daily.
Retrospection is a real powerful tool. Observable patterns and personal apparatus as leverage.

Go increase the size of the pie if you want a bigger share. Collaborative-growth over zero-sum. **2026-04 live evidence: this repo (agamarora/agamarora) is open-source as a direct application of this principle.**

Individual Contributor path is a valid career arc. People management is critical but not for everyone. **2026 reframe: AI-era amplifies IC leverage — IC + AI agents = throughput closer to a small team than ever.**
LinkedIn is a platform to be gamed productively. Posting IS the thinking. The loop closes when others learn (build → post → teach → learn → build).
Build to validate; don't just conceptualize. The prototype is the argument. (Absorbs former tinker-publicly belief as loop-closure framing per A3.)
Breadth of knowledge across domains is a professional differentiator.

If your breadth has no depth, you are what one could call a Human-GPT. Choose your niche and invest. **Humanness-as-depth extension is Agam's synthesis (April 2024 EQ post predates September 2024 Mo Gawdat citation).**
ChatGPT plugins were the first AI marketplace. Early 2023 seed of the serving-lens agent thesis.
Agents should be kept away from decision-making unless evaluable programmatically; safety is an application problem.
Decouple tokens from decisions; GIT-style reversibility at orchestration layer.
The model was not broken, the agent layer was; security vulnerabilities migrated from model weights to agent orchestration.
Prompts are plumbing inside the agent: guardrails, tool-calling, output parsing. Not user-facing UX.
We need to kill prompting. (Stance evolved per A1: prompting → context engineering → harness engineering. Verdict held; layer migrated.)
ICO is a fundraising tool, not a product.
Modern jobs will soon require AI tools as fluent as PowerPoint and Excel.
Frame the business problem first; let model selection follow.
The three durable enterprise AI moats are infrastructure, talent, and data. The model is not on the list.
AI-ready data means continuous collection and transformation systems, not just a large historical corpus.
It isn't the tool, it's the habit of being mindful with time.
You are a brand, and you should have your own nameplate. Ship it.
Product management is a featherless hat — omnipresent yet indistinguishable if done properly.
Building a product is like raising a child — make tough decisions, protect from distortion.
Correlation is not causation. Measurement discipline as PM fundamental.
Lean Startup loop. Smallest feature set, measure, iterate.
Value, Consumers, Scalability — three-pillar validation frame.
Non-functional requirements are not tick boxes but primary PM work. (Renamed from belief.non-functional-reqs-are-55pct-of-failure; 55% Standish citation dropped per E4.)
Design thinking is stakeholder alignment + diverse feedback, not UI/UX first. Jupyter notebook + paper wireframe as feedback loop.
I have a very strong opinion about having no strong opinions. Paradox-as-voice. **Explicit named meta-belief on pm-taste page per A2 — licenses every other belief on that page.**
Certifications fuel personal achievement but do not validate competence. Peer-voted badges acceptable; exam-gated certs rejected.
Deliberate practice via repetition of frameworks (re-watch, re-read) over novelty.
When picking your next role, optimize for the role after that. Routine career heuristic Agam deploys in mentoring. **Elevated from peripheral to named belief tier per F2.**
Never be the smartest person in the room; if you feel you are, get more people in the room or change the room. **Demoted from co-root to standard belief per F4. Scope: team-joining heuristic.**

R4 DEMOTE: single-surface, never restated in 12 years.
R4 MERGE → ship-the-prototype as loop-closure framing per A3. Slug retired.
External behavioral observation, not a belief Agam holds. Re-homed as style signal in voice-spec.
R4 DEMOTE: single-surface, subsumed into idea-validation-3-pillars.
RENAMED at lock to `belief.llm-as-primary-daily-tool` per B2. Old slug retired. Statement reframed (drop "extension of self" mysticism).
RENAMED at lock to `belief.non-functional-reqs-are-dominant-failure-mode` per E4. Old slug retired. 55% Standish citation dropped.

nodes.snippet: I am extremely opinionated about adding customization to products. To the point where I feel like I've lost a hard-fought battle with myself if a PRD ends up including customization settings. My brain is wired to jump to the long-term horizon where today's 'super customizable' product eventually requires six months of implementation, an implementation partner, hundreds of training documents, and a roadmap item just to enable a true day-zero go-live.
